acceptance_targets: [ch15-prose-001] PASS if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:304 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 uses complete clauses with explicit subjects and verbs for each image beat while preserving the fast reveal through the door; FAIL if the passage still relies on noun-phrase fragments.

[ch15-prose-002] PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:102, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:308, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:322, no more than two Teresa reaction beats use the mask-slip or mask-return construction and at least one later beat shows pressure through a different register such as misplacing an object, over-correcting language, or issuing a visibly compromised order; FAIL if each new Teresa setback still arrives through the same face-reset formula.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:304 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 uses complete clauses with explicit subjects and verbs for each image beat while preserving the fast reveal through the door; FAIL if the passage still relies on noun-phrase fragments.
findings.problem: The glimpse into the next car switches into clipped noun-phrase narration: 'Rows of actual bunks. A mother lifting a paper cup... Naila near the aisle...' That violates the constitution's no-elliptical-clipping rule and briefly reads like shorthand rather than fully controlled close-third prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:304 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 into grammatically complete sentences while keeping the speed of the glance and the three image beats already on the page: bunks, the mother and child, and Naila's steady look.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:102, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:308, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:322, no more than two Teresa reaction beats use the mask-slip or mask-return construction and at least one later beat shows pressure through a different register such as misplacing an object, over-correcting language, or issuing a visibly compromised order; FAIL if each new Teresa setback still arrives through the same face-reset formula.
findings.problem: Teresa's reversals are described four times through the same mask-slip-then-reset facial beat: the smile clips into place, the polish leaves and returns, the color leaves and comes back as authority, then the smile returns again. The repetition turns a useful motif into a tic and flattens a character whose stress tells should vary more than this chapter allows.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the Teresa-pressure beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:102, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:308, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:322 so at least one later stress response lands through object handling, syntax, or command behavior instead of another facial-mask reset.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the novel's uncomfortable overlap of sanitation, state violence, labor dependence, and family love. When an edge case appears, prefer the version that keeps physical consequence, class coercion, and emotional contradiction visible on the page rather than the version that makes characters cleaner, nobler, or easier to forgive.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns away at the exact moment bodily, economic, or moral consequence should become legible, revise toward specificity. Omission is only valid when residue, aftermath, and changed behavior keep the pressure fully present.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms should mark emphasis, bureaucracy, class performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate dense procedural chapters with quieter aftermath, meals, night holds, confession scenes, and dark-comic institutional theater. No two consecutive chapters should rely on the same rhythm of scene count, dialogue density, or pressure delivery; vary between confrontation, drift, revelation, ritual preparation, humiliation, public spectacle, and stillness under pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, infrastructural, unsentimental, and specific about procedures without becoming jargon-drunk. Abstract thought should rise from physical consequence instead of replacing it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored primarily to Marisol's bodily labor, practical thought, and moral self-management, with any widening kept chapter-level and earned by scene design.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Let long freighted sentences carry labor process, institutional language, or accumulating sensory load; then cut to shorter, grammatically complete sentences at impact points where a body, fact, or decision can no longer be blurred.
